canonicalChoiceReason: User designated build-72-hour-kit as the 'keep' slug. It also has the cleaner, shorter slug and more source references with real URLs (4 vs 2). Both are published.
layerMismatchNote: The canonical guide is layer:scenario / scenario_guide; the archive guide is layer:preparedness / preparedness_guide. The merged guide adopts the canonical's layer (preparedness) and guideType (preparedness_guide) since the content is fundamentally a preparedness checklist. This is a deliberate upgrade from scenario to preparedness framing, keeping the richer checklist-style content.

fieldDecisions.source: build-72-hour-kit: 'Build a 72-Hour Emergency Kit' — cleaner, action-verb phrasing
Content is a preparedness checklist; 'preparedness' layer fits better than 'scenario'
fieldDecisions.note: Guide B was preparedness; Guide A was scenario. Merged guide uses preparedness since this is a kit-building guide.

fieldDecisions.source: Combined specificity from both guides into one concise summary
Merged actionable detail from both guides

Archive guide's version ('Build one centralized kit sized for everyone in the household for at least 72 hours') is more specific than canonical ('Build a ready-to-grab kit with enough core supplies for at least several days')
Canonical had null; archive had a useful fallback about starting small
7 steps from archive + 2 unique steps from canonical (maps/tools, whistle), deduplicated and reordered to 9 total
fieldDecisions.note: Archive had more granular steps; canonical added maps, phone charging, basic tools, and whistle. Combined and capped under 10.

4 from canonical (all real URLs) + 2 from archive (real URLs). Deduplicated by organization+topic to 4 unique refs.

Use 'preparedness_guide' to match the preparedness layer
